checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?
checks.guidance: Cross-check the result against explicit user notes, critique corrections, and watch items. Flag anything the implementation ignored, contradicted, or only partially covered.

findings.detail: Checked the milestone brief against the actual overlay surface in `src/shared/components/ui/*` and the plan's proposed migration list. The repo still has a portal-bearing `hover-card.tsx` built on `@base-ui/react/preview-card` with a hardcoded `z-[100001]`, and that component is used in live lightbox-adjacent flows such as `AdjacentSegmentNavigation.tsx`, `ConstituentImageNavigation.tsx`, and `VariantCard.tsx`; the plan raises this in metadata as an open question instead of resolving whether it is intentionally out of scope, so the centralization brief is only partially addressed.

Checked the user requirement that z-index ownership stop being hardcoded across files, not just inside wrappers. Current call sites still override overlay layering directly through props and classes, for example `LoraSelectorModal.tsx` uses `overlayClassName="!z-[100012]"` and `zIndexBase={UI_Z_LAYERS.LIGHTBOX_MODAL + 2}`, `sharedSections.tsx` passes `className="z-[100001]"` to `SelectContent`, and many `TooltipContent` callers in the lightbox and variant selector pass `z-[100001]` or `z-[100010]`; the plan does not make those call-site overrides a first-class cleanup target, so it only partially satisfies the approved requirement that layering flow through the central stack.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?
checks.guidance: Look for logic errors, invalid assumptions, broken invariants, schema mismatches, or behavior that would fail at runtime. When the fix adds a conditional branch, check whether it handles all relevant cases — not just the one reported in the issue.

findings.detail: Checked the current nested-modal path in `LoraSelectorModal.tsx`. That caller depends on `DialogContent`'s `zIndexBase` and `overlayClassName` escape hatches to sit above the lightbox, and it closes itself only when `getTopDialogElement() === contentRef.current`; the plan says those z-index escape hatches should probably be removed and wrapper-local layering should come from the new stack, but it never explains how this specific caller will keep its above-lightbox ordering or how the top-dialog check will continue to work once the rendered topmost element may no longer be the same content node reference.

Checked the existing lightbox interaction logic in `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts`, `useLightboxNavigation.ts`, and `uiLayers.ts`. Those paths currently gate outside-click and Escape behavior by scanning DOM elements marked with `data-dialog-backdrop`, `data-dialog-content`, and `data-lightbox-popup` and comparing computed z-index values; if M2 introduces `overlayStack` as the new source of truth but leaves these DOM-scanning guards in place, the app will have two competing overlay-order mechanisms, which is a correctness risk rather than just an implementation detail.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?
checks.guidance: Look at how the changed function is used across the codebase. Does the fix only address one caller's scenario while others remain broken? Flag missing required work or out-of-scope edits. A minimal patch is often right, but check whether the underlying problem is bigger than what the issue describes.

findings.detail: Searched for other portal-based overlay primitives and found that the broader repo pattern extends beyond the six Base UI families named in the implementation plan. `hover-card.tsx` wraps `@base-ui/react/preview-card`, portals through its own `Positioner`, and hardcodes a z-index, so the underlying 'centralize portal ownership' problem is broader than the plan's current wrapper list even though the brief text only explicitly named dialog/popover/select/menu/tooltip/alert-dialog/context-menu/popup.

Searched for overlay consumers rather than just primitive definitions and found heavy layering customizations in downstream callers, especially under `src/domains/media-lightbox/` and `src/shared/components/VariantSelector/`. The plan's assumption that most consumers can stay untouched behind compatibility re-exports is optimistic because many of the current behavior differences live in those callers through `container`, `className`, and z-index overrides, so the broader issue is not confined to moving raw Base UI imports into one folder.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?
checks.guidance: Search for all instances of the symbol/pattern being changed. Also ask: does this feature require setup, registration, or integration code beyond the core logic? Missing glue code causes test failures even when the core fix is correct.

findings.detail: Checked the supporting infrastructure around overlay ordering and found non-wrapper code that the plan does not explicitly migrate: `src/shared/lib/uiLayers.ts` and `uiLayers.test.ts`, `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts`, `useLightboxNavigation.ts`, and `LoraSelectorModal.tsx` all encode dialog precedence in terms of DOM markers and fixed z-layers. Without naming those files as part of the migration, the repo will still contain a second overlay-order system after `overlayStack` lands.

Checked the test surface and confirmed the existing automated coverage is currently limited to `dialog.test.tsx`, `select.test.tsx`, `tooltipPrimitives.test.tsx`, and `uiLayers.test.ts`, with no existing menu integration file. The plan does identify the need for new stack and menu/dialog tests, so the testing direction is reasonable, but the supporting `uiLayers` tests also need either replacement or deliberate preservation because they currently codify the old DOM-z-index strategy.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?
checks.guidance: Grep for call sites. For each caller, check what values it passes — especially edge cases like None, zero, empty, or composite inputs. Then ask: should this change be here, or in a caller, callee, or new method?

findings.detail: Checked the callers of the current overlay wrappers and found that several of them pass nontrivial arguments that the migration has to preserve exactly: `SelectContent` is passed `container={container}` in LoRA and lightbox edit-mode flows so the popup portals into the correct subtree, `PopoverContent` is also passed `container` in selectors and AI input controls, and `DialogContent` is passed custom `style`, `overlayClassName`, and `zIndexBase` in modal flows. The plan says 'keep prop API identical' but only explicitly calls out `command.tsx` and `LightboxShell`, so it does not yet show how these concrete caller contracts will be handled once layering is centralized.

Checked type-only callers of Base UI and verified that `src/shared/components/ui/command.tsx` imports `@base-ui/react/dialog` only to derive `DialogPrimitiveForProps.Root` props for `CommandDialog`. The plan correctly identifies this file as an adapter-only caller that should switch to wrapper-exported prop types, so this part of the caller surface appears understood.

flags.concern: Overlay layering: Recurring debt: call-site z-index overrides remain outside the migration contract, so central wrapper ownership would still be bypassed by `TooltipContent`, `SelectContent`, and `DialogContent` consumers that currently pass `z-[...]`, `overlayClassName`, or `zIndexBase`.
flags.evidence: Examples include `src/domains/lora/components/LoraSelectorModal/LoraSelectorModal.tsx:111-112`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/editModes/sharedSections.tsx:163,231`, and many `TooltipContent` callers such as `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/ButtonGroups.tsx:69,96,195,225` and `src/shared/components/ProfitSplitBar.tsx:38,56,74`.

flags.concern: Lightbox overlay ownership: the plan does not explicitly retire the existing DOM-z-index precedence helpers, leaving `overlayStack` and `uiLayers` as competing sources of truth for which overlay is topmost.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/lib/uiLayers.ts` computes topmost dialogs from `[data-lightbox-popup], [data-dialog-content], [data-dialog-backdrop]`, and that logic is consumed by `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useLightboxNavigation.ts`, and `src/domains/lora/components/LoraSelectorModal/LoraSelectorModal.tsx`.

flags.concern: Overlay scope: the repository still has a live portal-bearing `preview-card` wrapper in `hover-card.tsx`, so the plan's current primitive list may leave one of the same portal ownership mechanisms outside the hardening pass.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/components/ui/hover-card.tsx` wraps `@base-ui/react/preview-card` with a portal and `className="z-[100001]"`, and it is used by lightbox and variant-selection surfaces such as `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/layouts/AdjacentSegmentNavigation.tsx` and `src/shared/components/VariantSelector/components/VariantCard.tsx`.

flags.concern: Criterion 8: requires human verification (drive_browser, inspect_runtime_ui, verify_physical_device).
